cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia With AI Chip Sales
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its custom AI chips to other data centers, representing a potential $50 billion opportunity. CEO Andy Jassy sees this as a direct challenge to Nvidia's market dominance.

cards.title: FERC Fast-Tracks Data Center Grid Access
cards.body: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission ordered grid operators to prioritize AI data center interconnections. The move failed to address underlying electricity supply shortages.

cards.title: Slowtech Movement Reclaims User Attention
cards.body: A new wave of technology prioritizes user control over attention and time. People increasingly reject constant engagement in favor of tools that support personal autonomy.

cards.title: Karamo Brown Launches AI Wellness App
cards.body: The "Queer Eye" life coach unveiled Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone. The app draws from Brown's personal journey through fitness, meditation, and personal growth.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $300M Funding
cards.body: The embodied AI startup is in talks for $300M at a $2B valuation. General Intuition trains world models using Medal's dataset of 2 billion videos annually.

cards.title: Tech Workers Launch $5M Political PAC
cards.body: Guardrails PAC positions itself as a populist alternative to Big Tech's political spending. The group runs on small donations from workers in the AI industry.

cards.title: Pixi Transforms Messaging With Interactive AR
cards.body: Pixi's new iOS app replaces traditional stickers and emoji reactions with interactive augmented reality. The company bets that AR is messaging's next evolution.

cards.title: How to Disable Google Docs AI Features
cards.body: Google's Gemini pop-ups in Docs can now be turned off. The guide shows users how to remove the "write with Gemini" feature.

cards.title: Roelof Botha Joins SpaceX Board
cards.body: The former Sequoia Capital leader fills an existing vacancy on SpaceX's board. The appointment comes days after SpaceX's landmark IPO.

cards.title: Snap's Expensive AR Glasses Tank Stock
cards.body: Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut has disappointed investors. The company's stock took a dive following the product's high price point reveal.

cards.title: Enterprise AI ROI Concerns Mount Rapidly
cards.body: Companies like Uber burned through annual AI budgets in months during the tokenmaxxing craze. Organizations are now reassessing AI spending as actual ROI bills arrive.

cards.title: Global Leaders Fear US AI Control
cards.body: France and India raised alarms at the G7 summit that America could cut off AI access overnight. The Anthropic blackout recently made this geopolitical fear tangible.

cards.title: Anthropic Joins Carbon Removal Coalition
cards.body: Anthropic became the first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition. The coalition received an additional $915M in pledges for carbon removal projects.

cards.title: Users Gain Algorithm Control on Platforms
cards.body: Threads, Instagram, and TikTok now let users directly influence their recommendation algorithms. Social media feeds are becoming more customizable and user-controlled.

cards.title: Odyssey World Model Reaches $1.45B Valuation
cards.body: The world model startup secured backing from Amazon and other major investors. Odyssey is positioning itself as a leading player beyond LLMs.

cards.title: Americans Skeptical About AI's Societal Impact
cards.body: Only 16 percent of Americans believe AI will positively impact society, according to Pew Research. Public optimism contrasts sharply with Wall Street enthusiasm.

cards.title: Google Home Speaker Gets Gemini Upgrade
cards.body: Google's new $99.99 Home Speaker replaces rigid voice commands with conversational Gemini interactions. The company is betting generative AI reinvigorates the smart speaker market.

cards.title: Robot Training Data Firms Emerge
cards.body: Companies like XDOF are collecting physical AI training data for major labs. Solving the robot data problem is critical for physical AI's next breakthrough.

cards.title: Pramaana Labs Raises $27M for AI Verification
cards.body: Khosla Ventures backed Pramaana's seed round to bring formal verification to AI systems. The company focuses on high-stakes verticals like law, drug discovery, and tax preparation.

cards.title: Amazon challenges Nvidia with AI chip sales
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers, representing a $50 billion opportunity. CEO Andy Jassy sees this as a major revenue stream for the company.

cards.title: AI data centers get fast-track grid access
cards.body: FERC mandated that grid operators give data centers priority for interconnections. However, the ruling fails to address critical electricity supply shortages.

cards.title: Karamo Brown launches wellness app with AI clone
cards.body: The "Queer Eye" star has created Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone. The app guides users through fitness, meditation, and personal growth.

cards.title: General Intuition raises $300M for world models
cards.body: The startup trains embodied AI and world models using Medal's dataset of 2 billion videos annually. It's valued at around $2 billion.

cards.title: How to disable AI features in Google Docs
cards.body: Google's "write with Gemini" pop-ups are bothering users. Here's how to turn off AI assistance in your Google Docs.

cards.title: Enterprises struggle with AI ROI calculations
cards.body: After pushing "tokenmaxxing" early in 2026, companies are now facing budget reality. Uber reportedly exhausted its annual AI budget in months.

cards.title: Global leaders fear US AI cutoff capability
cards.body: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 that America could disable AI access overnight. The Anthropic blackout validated these concerns.

cards.title: Anthropic joins carbon removal coalition
cards.body: Anthropic became the first AI startup to join the Frontier coalition. The group received another $915 million in pledges for carbon removal projects.

cards.title: Odyssey valued at $1.45B for world models
cards.body: The AI startup has secured major backing from Amazon and others. World models represent the next frontier beyond large language models.

cards.title: Only 16% of Americans optimistic about AI
cards.body: A new Pew Research study reveals public skepticism about AI's societal impact. This contrasts sharply with Wall Street's enthusiasm for the technology.

cards.title: Google Home Speaker powered by Gemini AI
cards.body: Google's new $99.99 speaker replaces rigid voice commands with conversational Gemini interactions. The device aims to reinvent the smart home speaker market.

cards.title: AI labs outsource robot training data collection
cards.body: Companies like XDOF provide the unglamorous work of collecting physical data for robotics. Solving this data problem is critical for physical AI advancement.

cards.title: Pramaana Labs raises $27M for AI verification
cards.body: Khosla Ventures backed the startup to bring formal verification to AI systems. Focus areas include law, drug discovery, and tax preparation.

cards.title: AI coding agents teach robots GPU installation
cards.body: Machine learning agents have trained robots to install GPUs and cut zip ties. This demonstrates AI's expanding role in physical automation.

cards.title: Dangerous AI models inevitable despite concerns
cards.body: Experts warn that advanced and risky AI models will continue developing regardless of regulatory efforts. The race for AI capabilities shows no signs of slowing.

cards.title: Google Home Speaker finally available for preorder
cards.body: Ten months after announcement, Google's $100 smart speaker powered by Gemini is finally taking preorders. The device aims to revolutionize voice-based home control.

cards.title: Why South Korea loves AI so much
cards.body: South Koreans have embraced AI adoption at remarkable rates, from immigration to daily services. The nation views AI as essential to its competitive future.

cards.title: Data centers need flexibility for grid stability
cards.body: AI data centers consume massive power; grid operators need flexible solutions. Balancing demand can prevent blackouts during peak usage periods.

cards.title: Military AI advisors reshape defense decisions
cards.body: An MIT Technology Review eBook explores how militaries deploy AI models for decision-making. The exclusive collection updates stories from April 2025 through 2026.
